zh.lifeSummary: 當James III Stewart King of Scotland於1451年7月10日出生在蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林時，他的父親James II Stewart King of Scotland20歲，母親Maria van Gueldres20歲。他於1469年7月13日與Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg在蘇格蘭艾丁波結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1488年登記服兵役。他去世於1488年6月11日在蘇格蘭斯林史爾聖 尼尼安斯享年36歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭斯林史爾坎布斯肯尼斯 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James III Stewart King of Scotland于1451年7月10日出生在苏格兰斯林史爾斯特林时，他的父亲James II Stewart King of Scotland20岁，母亲Maria van Gueldres20岁。他于1469年7月13日与Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg在苏格兰艾丁波结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他于1488年登记服兵役。他去世于1488年6月11日在苏格兰斯林史爾聖 尼尼安斯享年36岁，埋葬在苏格兰斯林史爾坎布斯肯尼斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When James III Stewart King of Scotland was born on 10 July 1451, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, his father, James II Stewart King of Scotland, was 20 and his mother, Maria van Gueldres, was 20. He married Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg on 13 July 1469, in Edinburgh, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He registered for military service in 1488. He died on 11 June 1488, in Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 36, and was buried in Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James III Stewart King of Scotland est né le 10 juillet 1451, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, son père, James II Stewart King of Scotland, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Maria van Gueldres, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg le 13 juillet 1469, à Edinburgh, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1488. Il est décédé le 11 juin 1488, à Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterré à Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als James III Stewart King of Scotland am 10. Juli 1451 in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater James II Stewart King of Scotland 20 und seine Mutter Maria van Gueldres 20. Er hat am 13. Juli 1469 in Edinburgh, Scotland Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er hat sich 1488 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 11. Juni 1488 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Scotland und wurde in Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James III Stewart King of Scotland è nato il il 10 luglio 1451 nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, il suo padre, James II Stewart King of Scotland, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Maria van Gueldres, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg il 13 luglio 1469, nel Edinburgh, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1488. Morì il 11 giugno 1488, nel Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolto a Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: James III Stewart King of Scotlandが1451年7月10日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアスティルリングで生まれた時，父James II Stewart King of Scotlandは20歳，母Maria van Gueldresは20歳であった。1469年7月13日にスコットランドエディンブルグでMargaret Of Denmark Oldenburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1488年に兵役登録をした。彼は1488年6月11日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアサイント ニニアンスで36歳で死去し，スコットランドスティルリングシュアイアカンブスケネス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James III Stewart King of Scotland(이)가 1451년 7월 10일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James II Stewart King of Scotland(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria van Gueldres(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1469년 7월 13일 스컷랜드 에딘버그에서 Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1488년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1488년 6월 11일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 세인트 니니누스에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 캄버스켄스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.10 July 1451–11 June 1488: 10 de julho de 1451–11 de junho de 1488
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James III Stewart King of Scotland nasceu em 10 de julho de 1451, em Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, seu pai, James II Stewart King of Scotland, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Maria van Gueldres, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg em 13 de julho de 1469, em Edinburgh, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1488. Ele faleceu em 11 de junho de 1488, em Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Scotland, com 36 anos, e foi sepultado em Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James III Stewart King of Scotland родился 10 июля 1451 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени James II Stewart King of Scotland было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria van Gueldres, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg 13 июля 1469 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. В 1488 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 11 июня 1488 г. в местности сеинт нинянс, стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности камбускеннет эбби, стирлингшир, Шотландия.

es.10 July 1451–11 June 1488: 10 de julio de 1451–11 de junio de 1488
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James III Stewart King of Scotland nació el 10 de julio de 1451, en Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, su papá, James II Stewart King of Scotland, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Maria van Gueldres, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg el 13 de julio de 1469, en Edinburgh, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1488. Murió el 11 de junio de 1488, en Saint Ninians, Stirlingshire, Scotland, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultado en Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland.
